title: ophrys amanensis subsp. iceliensis
intro: Ophrys amanensis subsp. iceliensis is a subspecies of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula and some parts of North Africa, commonly known as the Icely bee orchid due to its unique appearance resembling a female bee. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ophrys amanensis subsp. iceliensis include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its roots are used to make infusions and decoctions.
